reasoning_model_1: The user requested the sentence 'This thing, I must get it.' with stress on the word 'I' and normal volume. Model 1 generated the audio with the content 'This thing, I must get.', omitting the final word 'it'. This constitutes a content accuracy error (Step 1 of evaluation criteria). Regarding the paralinguistic feature of Stress (Dimension 1), the omission of the final word alters the prosody and rhythm of the sentence, making it impossible to perfectly convey the intended stress pattern within the correct context. While the word 'I' may have been emphasized, the structural failure limits the score to 1 (Major issues). Regarding Volume (Dimension 2), the audio appears to be at a normal level consistent with the 'normal volume' instruction, meriting a score of 3.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 generated the audio with the content 'This thing, I must get it.', which perfectly matches the requested script (Content Accuracy). Regarding Stress (Dimension 1), the model correctly places emphasis on the subject 'I' ('This thing, *I* must get it'), effectively contrasting the speaker with others or emphasizing determination, fulfilling the core paralinguistic requirement naturally. A score of 3 is assigned. Regarding Volume (Dimension 2), the speech is delivered at a consistent, normal conversational volume as requested, earning a score of 3.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the fundamental requirement of Content Accuracy by omitting the word 'it' at the end of the sentence. This content error negatively impacts the evaluation of the Stress dimension, as the sentence structure was compromised. Model 2 followed the script perfectly and successfully applied the requested stress on 'I' while maintaining natural fluency. Both models performed equally well on the Volume dimension, adhering to the 'normal volume' instruction.
